A.Description: Adequate studies show no risk to the fetus in the first trimester

B.Description: Animal studies show no risk, or animal studies show risk but human studies do not

C.Description: Animal studies show risk or no studies available. Benefit may outweigh risk

D.Description: Evidence of human fetal risk. Benefit may outweigh risk in life-threatening situations

X.Description: Proven fetal risk. Contraindicated in pregnancy

L1.Description: Drug has been taken by a large number of breastfeeding mothers without any observed increase in adverse effects in the infant

L2.Description: Drug has been studied in a limited number of breastfeeding mothers without adverse effects in the infant

L3.Description: No controlled studies in breastfeeding women; however, the risk of untoward effects to a breastfed infant is possible

L4.Description: Positive evidence of risk to a breastfed infant or breast milk production

L5.Description: Significant risk to the infant, or the drug has a high potential for causing serious damage

Warfarin.notes: Anticoagulant. Avoid in pregnancy except mechanical heart valves.

Lisinopril.notes: ACE inhibitor. Linked to fetal renal failure.

Magnesium Sulfate.notes: Used for preeclampsia prevention. Monitor reflexes.
